lines.speaker: greta
lines.text: You really think someone's poisoning the well?

lines.speaker: elara
lines.text: I KNOW someone is. The question is who benefits from a dead village.

lines.speaker: greta
lines.text: Everyone with a grudge and a bottle of nightcap, apparently.

lines.speaker: gate_guard
lines.text: Another sermon today. Third this week.

lines.speaker: villager
lines.text: My wife went. Says he's right — the crops dying IS punishment.

lines.speaker: gate_guard
lines.text: Punishment for what? Being poor?

lines.speaker: villager_1
lines.text: The Miller family left yesterday. Just packed up and walked east.

lines.speaker: villager_2
lines.text: Can't blame them. Three children and nothing to feed them.

lines.speaker: villager_1
lines.text: If the lord doesn't act soon, we'll all be walking.

lines.speaker: villager
lines.text: Who would burn the granary? That was everything we had.

lines.speaker: greta
lines.text: Someone who wants us desperate. And it's working.

lines.speaker: gate_guard
lines.text: Ironmarch riders on the east road again. Third time this week.

lines.speaker: villager
lines.text: They're counting our walls. Measuring us for a coffin.

lines.speaker: gate_guard
lines.text: We don't even HAVE walls worth counting.

lines.speaker: old_maren
lines.text: The roots are closer today. Can you feel them?

lines.speaker: elara
lines.text: I can see them. They broke through the eastern fence overnight.

lines.speaker: old_maren
lines.text: The forest is tired of waiting. It's coming to collect.

lines.speaker: greta
lines.text: Did you hear? The priest — he's Old Maren's grandson.

lines.speaker: gate_guard
lines.text: Silas's boy? The one the Church took?

lines.speaker: greta
lines.text: The same. And Elara's his sister. She doesn't know yet.

lines.speaker: gate_guard
lines.text: God help this village. We break everything we touch.

lines.speaker: fenrick
lines.text: The last three leave tomorrow. Before dawn.

lines.speaker: unknown
lines.text: The youngest won't stop crying for her mother.

lines.speaker: fenrick
lines.text: ...Tell her mother's waiting at the end of the road. It's kinder than the truth.

lines.speaker: villager_1
lines.text: Think it'll rain?

lines.speaker: villager_2
lines.text: It should. It always rains in autumn.

lines.speaker: villager_1
lines.text: Not this autumn. Nothing's right this autumn.

lines.speaker: villager
lines.text: Your chickens got into my garden again.

lines.speaker: farmer
lines.text: What garden? Everything's dead.

lines.speaker: villager
lines.text: ...Fair point.

lines.speaker: villager
lines.text: The bread tastes wrong.

lines.speaker: greta
lines.text: The flour tastes wrong. The water tastes wrong. Everything tastes wrong.

lines.speaker: villager
lines.text: I miss when things tasted right.

lines.speaker: child
lines.text: Why does Anchor sit at the forge every day?

lines.speaker: villager
lines.text: He's waiting for the blacksmith to come back.

lines.speaker: child
lines.text: Will he come back?

lines.speaker: villager
lines.text: ...Eat your turnips.

lines.speaker: villager_1
lines.text: My boots are falling apart.

lines.speaker: villager_2
lines.text: The cobbler left last week.

lines.speaker: villager_1
lines.text: Before or after the baker?

lines.speaker: villager_2
lines.text: After. Everyone leaves after the baker.

lines.speaker: villager
lines.text: Can't sleep. The ground hums at night.

lines.speaker: gate_guard
lines.text: That's just the wind.

lines.speaker: villager
lines.text: Wind doesn't have a rhythm, Harlan.

lines.speaker: child_1
lines.text: I'm the forest! You have to run!

lines.speaker: child_2
lines.text: I don't want to play Roots and Runners anymore.

lines.speaker: child_1
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: child_2
lines.text: Because last time, the trees played back.

lines.speaker: villager
lines.text: Did you go to the sermon?

lines.speaker: villager_2
lines.text: Twice. He says the crops are punishment.

lines.speaker: villager
lines.text: Punishment for what?

lines.speaker: villager_2
lines.text: He wasn't specific. That's the scary part.

lines.speaker: villager
lines.text: The stars look different tonight.

lines.speaker: old_maren
lines.text: The stars haven't changed. Your eyes have. Fear does that.

lines.speaker: villager_1
lines.text: The Millers left. And the Thatchers.

lines.speaker: villager_2
lines.text: How many is that now?

lines.speaker: villager_1
lines.text: Eleven families. Forty-three people.

lines.speaker: villager_2
lines.text: Forty-three. And the lord still hasn't said a word.

lines.speaker: villager
lines.text: Remember the harvest festival?

lines.speaker: greta
lines.text: Three years ago. The last good one.

lines.speaker: villager
lines.text: My daughter danced. She was six.

lines.speaker: greta
lines.text: Where is she now?

lines.speaker: villager
lines.text: Sent her to my sister in the east. Before the roads got dangerous.

lines.speaker: villager
lines.text: I keep hearing the forge. Hammer on anvil.

lines.speaker: harlan
lines.text: The forge is cold. Has been for months.

lines.speaker: villager
lines.text: I know. I hear it anyway.
